title: [FIRRTL][LowerLayers] Add inner symbols directly when possible
body:
When lowering layer blocks, add inner symbols directly to operations that
support them (via InnerSymbolOpInterface) instead of always creating
intermediate nodes. This reduces unnecessary indirection in the output.

Operations that can support inner symbols and have a single result
(or target a specific result): get symbols added directly. For operations
that don't support inner symbols or have multiple results without
targeting a specific result, a node is created and RAUW is performed to
maintain correct behavior.

This continues to use a node for instance port captures.  This could be
changed in a future commit or at least made consistent with `LowerXMR` by
doing a partial lowering of captures to probes _before_ `LowerXMR` and
then relying on consistent lowering by `LowerXMR`.

This change also refactors the node cache to use NodeOp instead of
Operation* for better type safety.

Change the `LowerLayers` pass to suffix all created nodes with
`_layer_probe`.  This provides better provenance of why a specific node
was created as opposed to relying on the generic `ExportVerilog` `_T`
suffixing which is highly ambiguous.

AI-assisted-by: Augment (Claude Sonnet 4.5)
